name: The Cloudbank
image_prompt: A surreal open-air salon resting on top of a solid cloud at perpetual sunset. The cloud floor is soft pearlescent white, glowing faintly from underneath, extending outward like a small island in the sky. Wicker armchairs and a low brass tea table arranged in a conversational grouping. Paper-and-brass lanterns strung on iron poles driven into the cloud, glowing warm orange. A planter of jasmine with no visible soil. The horizon glows in three bands: deep rose near the cloud floor, turquoise in the middle, violet above, with early stars pricking the violet. No building, no walls, only open sky in every direction. Empty, no people. Colors: pearlescent white cloud, rose pink, turquoise, violet twilight, warm lantern amber. Magical realist storybook atmosphere.
location: A salon perched on the flat top of a cloud that has refused to drift for over a century. The cloud sits two thousand feet above an empty stretch of ocean nobody has charted. Access is by a folding wooden staircase that descends from the cloud's underside whenever someone is expected, and retracts when they are not.
time: Just after sunset, indefinitely. The horizon glows in three bands: a deep rose at the bottom, a turquoise band above it, and a violet vault above that. The first stars are visible but never become more than the first stars.
weather: The air is summer-warm and smells of high altitude. A breeze moves across the cloud surface but the cloud itself does not move. Below the salon's edge, weather happens to other clouds; up here, the weather is always exactly this.

atmosphere.lighting: Lanterns made of paper and brass strung between iron poles driven into the cloud as if into solid ground. The lanterns light themselves at dusk and have done so every dusk for as long as the salon has existed. The cloud floor itself glows faintly from beneath, a soft pearlescence underfoot.
atmosphere.sound: Wind that carries no sound from the world below. A faint ringing far overhead that might be high atmospheric ice or might be something singing. The salon's own sounds are clear and immediate: cup against saucer, the creak of wicker, the rustle of paper.
atmosphere.crowd: The salon feels intimate no matter who is on it. The cloud accommodates. Conversations carry without effort because there is nothing for the sound to lose itself in.
atmosphere.smell: Ozone, the cold mineral scent of high altitude, a thread of jasmine from a planter that has no soil, fresh tea steeped from a leaf nobody can identify, and beneath it all a faint salt from the unseen ocean far below

layout.main_terrace: A wide platform of bleached teak floorboards laid directly on the cloud, stable underfoot, the boards warm even at altitude. A long low table at the center surrounded by wicker chairs and floor cushions of indigo velvet. Tea service on a brass tray. The horizon is visible from every chair.
layout.stargazing_couch: A long curved daybed at the eastern edge, upholstered in faded gold, oriented to face the first stars. A wool blanket folded at one end. Lying on it tilts the head exactly toward the constellation that has the most to say tonight.
layout.writing_alcove: A small octagonal pavilion at the western edge, walls of stretched silk, a low writing desk inside with paper and ink and a single brass lamp. The silk filters the lantern light into a rose glow. Writing here yields letters that arrive at their addressees a day before they were sent.
layout.edge_walk: A path of teak planks circling the cloud's perimeter with a low rope handrail. Walking it feels exactly as safe and exactly as not safe as walking on a cloud should feel. The view changes as you walk: ocean below, then ocean below, then ocean below, but each view is somehow a different ocean.
layout.balloon_anchor: A single iron ring driven into the cloud at the southern edge with a length of mooring rope coiled neatly beside it. There has never been a balloon. The rope is always slightly damp.

objects_of_note: A brass orrery on the main table, the planets in correct positions for tonight, the model itself rotating at its own slow pace without any visible mechanism.
A silver bell on a stand near the staircase, used to signal the staircase to descend. The bell has not been struck since whoever struck it last and the salon is open anyway.
A single tarot card face down on the corner of the table. Nobody turns it over. Everybody plans to.
A weather vane on a pole at the cloud's center pointing in a direction that does not match the wind. It points the same way every visit, and the direction does not correspond to any compass heading.
A guest book bound in indigo leather on a small stand near the staircase, the entries handwritten in many languages, the most recent entry undated.
mood: Suspended and clarifying. The altitude lifts everything that came up the staircase: deadlines, status, the weight of being right. The horizon is far enough away that scale resets and small problems look small. Disagreements thin out at this elevation; what survives the climb is what actually matters. The salon does not solve anything. It removes the noise that was hiding what was already known.
